definition: The breakdown of glucose using oxygen to produce ATP, CO₂, and water.
Tiny cavities in lungs where O₂ and CO₂ exchange occurs.
Different versions of the same gene (e.g., dominant vs. recessive).
The energy production without oxygen, producing lactate (animals) or ethanol (yeast).
A protein produced by the immune system to neutralize pathogens.
Reproduction without gametes (e.g., budding, binary fission).
Incorporation of absorbed nutrients into body tissues.
The energy currency of cells, storing and transferring chemical energy.
